agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"Kafka topic (payment.events)","to":"Payment Event Consumer","status":"risk","issue":"Offset is committed immediately upon receive, BEFORE processing. If the consumer crashes after commit but before processing completes (or before the event reaches retry logic), the event is silently lost. This converts the delivery guarantee from at-least-once to at-most-once, which is unacceptable for payment data."},{"from":"Payment Event Consumer","to":"Payment Database","status":"correct","issue":"Circuit breaker with 60s reset window is in place. Adequate for downstream protection."},{"from":"Payment Event Consumer","to":"Dead-Letter Topic (payment.events.dlq)","status":"risk","issue":"The DLQ is only useful if the event actually enters the retry pipeline. Because offset is committed before processing, a crash between commit and first processing attempt means the event never reaches retry logic and never reaches the DLQ. The DLQ provides a false sense of safety."}],"contract_compliance":"The at-most-once delivery semantic created by early offset commit violates the implicit contract of a payment system, where every event must be accounted for. Payment events that are silently dropped cannot be reconciled.","data_flow_integrity":"Compromised. The gap between offset commit and processing completion creates a window where events can be permanently lost with no trace in logs, metrics, or the DLQ. The system will report success (offset advanced) for work it never performed."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"adequate","gaps":["No logging occurs between offset commit and processing start — a crash in this window leaves zero evidence the event ever existed","Add a log line BEFORE offset commit (not after) that records the event ID and offset, so crash forensics can identify the gap"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"Good baseline: events_consumed_total, events_failed_total, consumer_lag_gauge cover the primary signals","gaps":["No metric for events received but not yet processed (in-flight count) — this would expose the at-most-once gap","Consider adding events_dlq_published_total to monitor DLQ inflow rate separately","No histogram for processing duration (p50/p95/p99) — only structured log duration exists, which is harder to alert on"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"Not mentioned — unknown whether distributed tracing (e.g., trace ID propagation from event producer to consumer to database) is implemented","gaps":["If no trace ID is propagated in event headers, correlating a payment event across producer, consumer, and database becomes manual log grep work at 3am","Recommend injecting/extracting trace context (W3C traceparent or similar) in Kafka headers"]},"debugging_capability":"Adequate for happy-path debugging via structured JSON logs. Severely inadequate for diagnosing silent event loss caused by the offset commit timing, because there is no observable signal when an event is lost in the commit-to-process gap."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"Consumer crashes after offset commit but before processing starts","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"CRITICAL: Move offset commit to AFTER successful processing (or after DLQ publish on final retry failure). This converts delivery to at-least-once. Make processing idempotent using event ID to handle redelivery safely."},{"scenario":"All 3 retries exhausted, event sent to DLQ","handling":"handled","recommendation":"Ensure DLQ has its own consumer/alerting and a documented reprocessing runbook. Verify DLQ publish itself is retried if it fails."},{"scenario":"Circuit breaker opens on payment database","handling":"partial","recommendation":"What happens to in-flight events while the circuit is open? If they immediately fail and enter retry, 3 retries at 500ms may all fire within the 60s reset window and exhaust retries before the DB recovers. Consider: retries should respect circuit breaker state, or use exponential backoff that spans beyond the 60s window."},{"scenario":"Kafka broker becomes unreachable","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Verify health endpoint degrades (returns 5xx) when consumer cannot reach Kafka. Confirm consumer reconnection logic and whether consumer_lag_gauge flatlines or errors in this state."},{"scenario":"DLQ topic is unavailable when publishing a failed event","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"If DLQ publish fails, the event is lost (offset already committed). Add retry/fallback for DLQ publishing itself, or log the full event payload as a last resort so it can be manually recovered."},{"scenario":"Poison pill event causes repeated crashes","handling":"partial","recommendation":"With current early-commit behavior, a poison pill is actually skipped silently (offset advances past it). After fixing to commit-after-process, ensure poison pills are detected and routed to DLQ rather than causing infinite reprocessing loops."}],"retry_config":"3 retries with 500ms fixed backoff. Acceptable for transient errors, but fixed backoff risks thundering herd under load. Consider exponential backoff (500ms, 1s, 2s). Also, retry budget must coordinate with circuit breaker reset window (60s) — currently retries will exhaust in 1.5s, well before circuit resets.","timeout_config":"Not specified. Processing timeout is unknown — a hung database call could block the consumer indefinitely, stalling the entire partition. Add an explicit processing timeout per event.","circuit_breakers":"Present on payment database with 60s reset. Adequate pattern, but needs coordination with retry timing as noted above. Confirm half-open state behavior and how many trial requests are allowed.","graceful_degradation":"Partial. Circuit breaker provides database protection, DLQ provides a failure sink. However, there is no backpressure mechanism — if processing is slow, consumer lag will grow unboundedly with no throttling or pause strategy."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Redeploy previous image, consumer resumes from last committed offset. This is a sound rollback plan, HOWEVER, because offsets are committed before processing, any events consumed by the new version before rollback are already committed and will NOT be reprocessed by the rolled-back version. Events in that window are lost.","monitoring_coverage":"Baseline metrics (consumed, failed, lag) are present. Gaps in processing duration histograms, in-flight count, and DLQ-specific metrics reduce operational visibility.","on_call_documentation":"Not mentioned. Recommend creating a runbook covering: how to reprocess DLQ events, how to reset consumer group offsets, how to identify lost events via offset gaps, circuit breaker behavior, and escalation paths for payment discrepancies.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":[],"new_queues_topics":["payment.events.dlq — verify this topic is pre-created with appropriate retention and partition count matching the source topic"],"config_changes":["Move offset commit strategy from auto/early-commit to manual commit after processing"],"infra_provisioning":[]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":false,"data_preserving":false,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"No database migration details provided. Review assumes the payment database schema already exists."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"Kafka offset committed before processing creates at-most-once delivery for payment events","reason":"This is a data-loss bug, not a nice-to-have. In a payment system, silently dropping events leads to financial discrepancies, failed reconciliation, and potential regulatory issues. The offset commit strategy must be changed to commit-after-process, and event processing must be made idempotent. The existing DLQ and retry mechanisms are rendered ineffective by this ordering issue — they cannot catch events lost to crashes in the commit-to-process window. This is a must-fix before production.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"integration_risk","issue":"Retry timing does not coordinate with circuit breaker reset window","reason":"3 retries × 500ms = 1.5s total retry budget. Circuit breaker reset is 60s. If the circuit opens, all retries will fail against the open circuit and the event goes to DLQ even though the database may recover 58.5s later. Retries should use exponential backoff or respect circuit breaker state to avoid premature DLQ routing.","routed_to":"Coach K"}],"confidence":{"level":90,"high_confidence_areas":["The offset-before-processing issue is a well-understood anti-pattern with clear blast radius","Retry/circuit-breaker timing mismatch is deterministic and verifiable","Observability gaps are identifiable from the provided spec"],"low_confidence_areas":["Distributed tracing — not enough information to assess whether it exists","DLQ publish failure handling — implementation details not provided","Processing timeout configuration — not mentioned, may or may not exist"],"assumptions":["The service uses a standard Kafka consumer client with configurable offset commit behavior","The payment database circuit breaker is implemented at the application level","No other consumers share this consumer group","The DLQ topic has been created but its configuration is unverified"]}}
